GOBD | Blaise Diagne Intl | Dakar-Diass, Senegal

>> Go to this page.

GOBD Information

Blaise Diagne International Airport (French: Aéroport International Blaise Diagne) is an international airport near the town of Diass in Thiès Region, Senegal, 43 kilometres (27 mi) east of downtown Dakar. It serves as the main airport for Dakar, replacing Léopold Sédar Senghor International Airport (GOOY), which had become too small. It is named after Blaise Diagne, the first black African elected to France's parliament in 1914. More information available at Wikipedia.

Aerodrome Data

ICAO GOBD
IATA DSS
Elevation 290 ft
Transition Altitude 3500 ft
Transition Level FL40
Mag Variation 07º W
Coordinate 14°40′16″N 17°4′1″W
Site dakaraeroport.com

Apron VIP VIP Twy P -

GOBD Delivery

IFR Clearance Rules

* We have not implemented any specific IFR Clearance or Flight Plan Validation rules at this point.

Cruise Level Rules

* Senegal Airspace operates with the following RVSM standard table of Cruising Levels.

Magnetic Track 000º to 179º 180º to 359º
Flight Rules IFR VFR IFR VFR
Flight Level 010 - 020 -
030 035 040 045
050 055 060 065
070 075 080 085
090 095 100 105
110 115 120 125
130 135 140 145
150 - 160 -
170 - 180 -
190 - 200 -
210 - 220 -
230 - 240 -
250 - 260 -
270 - 280 -
290 - 310 -
330 - 350 -
370 - 390 -
410 - 430 -
450 - 470 -
490 - 510 -
530 - 550 -
570 - 590 -
etc - etc -
  • The VFR flights are not allowed over and above the flight level 150 (DOC 7030 ICAO).

Departure Procedures & Initial Climbs

  • GOBD does not have coded SID procedures, so, the runway heading rule will apply.
Rwy Heading Initial Climb Remarks
01 006 3000 ft -
19 186 3000 ft -
Please alert the pilots to the Noise Abatement Procedures

GOBD (Blaise Diagne Intl) | v0.1

>> Go to this page.
>> Go to this page.

GOBD Ground

Push-back Procedures

Phraseology
CALLSIGN, push-back approved, facing DIRECTION.
Directions
Apron Type Facing Remarks
Apron G General Aviation South -
Apron J1 Remote Stands West -
Apron J2 Remote Stands West -
Apron N Overflow/ Remote North/South -
Apron T Main Terminal North/South -
Apron V Cargo South -
Apron VIP VIP South -

Taxi Out Procedures

  • When runway 01 is active, departing traffic shall be instructed to taxi to the runway via C and hold short H, allowing Tower Controllers to issue further instructions to taxi to C6 or C7 based on preferential departure separation.
  • When runway 19 is active, departing traffic shall be instructed to taxi to the runway via C and contact Tower after passing A1, allowing Tower Controllers to issue further instructions to taxi to C1 or C2 based on preferential departure separation.
Phraseology
CALLSIGN, taxi via taxiway CHARLIE to holding point CHARLIE SEVEN.

Taxi In Procedures

  • In low traffic situations, Tower Controllers are expected to hand over arrival traffic to Ground Controllers once the traffic has vacated the runway.
  • In high traffic situations, in order to maintain the high speed exits clear of traffic, Tower Controllers may issue an initial taxi instruction to arrival traffic before handover to Ground Controllers.
  • If runway 19 is active and traffic is vacating C5, the following instruction may be issued by the Tower Controller.
  • The exception with this rule is traffic landing on runway 19 and wishing to vacate at the end to Apron V. In this situation, Tower Controllers will maintain control of the aircraft.
Phraseology
CALLSIGN, welcome to Dakar. Taxi to STAND ALPHA SEVEN via taxiway CHARLIE, ALPHA THREE.

GOBD (Blaise Diagne Intl) | v0.1

>> Go to this page.
>> Go to this page.

GOBD Tower

Runway Selection

  • Runway 01 is the preferred active runway, up to 10 kts of tailwind.

Runway Change Operations

  • Runway changes must be coordinate with existing DEL, GND, TWR, DEP and APP controllers.
  • If possible, hold aircraft on stand before a runway change operation.
  • If an aircraft is already taxying on Twy C, offer the pilot the option to use the existing active Rwy or to enter the runway for taxiway direction change.
  • If an aicraft as already passed an approach IAF fix, an APP or TWR controller should advise the pilot of the possibility of a runway change prior to the aircraft being on the final approach segment.

Wake Separation

Arrivals (nm)
Lead J H M L
J
H 6 4
M 7 5 5
L 8 6 5 5
Departures (min)
Lead J H M L
J
H 2
M 3 2
L 3 2 2

Missed Approach Procedures

HIRO

  • When HIRO Ops are in effect, Tower Controllers will only handover traffic to Ground Controllers after issuing initial taxi instructions to pilots to avoid runway exit congestion.
  • If possible, Tower Controllers should advise pilots on which exit they should expect to vacate.
Rwy Exit Taxi Instructions Remarks
01 C3 (High Speed) 
Taxi Right on CHARLIE
 -
C2 -
19 C5 (High Speed) 
Taxi Right on CHARLIE
 -
C6 -
C8 - Cargo Apron Only

VFR Operations

  • Pilots must have the latest Visual Approach Chart.
VFR Circuits
Rwy Turn Altitude Remarks
01 Left Up to 1000 AFL -
19 Right Up to 1000 AFL -

Rwy Sector Exit Procedure Altitude Remarks
01 N SW-N1-NK-NG 1500 ft -
NE E3-TS 1000 ft Left Circuit before E3
E E3-E4 1000 ft Left Circuit before E3
SE E3-S1 1000 ft Left Circuit before E3
S E3-SN 1000 ft Left Circuit before E3
W SW-W2 1000 ft -
19 N SW-N1-NK-NG 1500 ft -
NE E3-TS 1000 ft -
E E3-E4 1000 ft -
SE E3-S1 1000 ft -
S E3-SN 1000 ft -
W SW-W2 1000 ft -
VFR Arrivals
  • Pilots must report crossing each VRP unless otherwise instructed by the controller.
Sector Entry Procedure Altitude Remarks
N NG-NK-N1-SW 1500 ft -
W W2-SW 1000 ft -
NE/E E4-E3 1000 ft -
SE S1-E3 1000 ft -
S S2-E3 1000 ft -
VFR Transitions
Visual Reference Points
Code Designation VOR & Radial Distance Remarks
NG NOVEMBER GOLF DS 017º 20.8 -
N1 NOVEMBER ONE DS 329º 14.6 -
NK NOVEMBER KILO DS 357º 16.5 -
E3 ECHO THREE DS 085º 3.3 -
E4 ECHO FOUR DS 077º 20.8 -
S1 SIERRA ONE DS 124º 21.1 -
W2 WHISKEY TWO DS 290º 21.3 -
SW SIERRA WHISKEY DS 275º 6.2 -
E5 ECHO FIVE DS 047º 21.4 -
SN SIERRA NOVEMBER DS 163º 7.3 -
E2 ECHO TWO DS 165º 18.5 -
TS TANGO SIERRA DS 056º 8.8 -
SN and S2 are reserved for arrivals and departures to Saly Airfield.
E5 is reserved for arrivals and departures to This Military Aerodrome.

Helicopter Operations

  • TLOF is available on TWY M, next to the threshold of RWY 19.

GOBD (Blaise Diagne Intl) | v0.1

>> Go to this page.
>> Go to this page.

GOBD Departure

  • GOBD doesn't have a dedicated Departure Position, with Diass Approach (GOBD APP) assuming the role of Departure.

Departure Procedures

  • After departure, maintain aircraft on runway heading. You may issue further climb instructions (usually FL150).
  • If necessary, vector aircraft right (runway 01 departures) or left (runway 19 departures)

Noise Abatement Procedures

  • When take-off on RWY 01 with a clearance to turn right, the ACFT must maintain the RWY axis until 3000 ft before beginning right turn.
  • When take-off on RWY 19 with a clearance to turn right, the ACFT must maintain the RWY axis during 3 minutes before beginning right turn.

Coordinating GOBD & GOOY Departures

  • Both GOBD and GOOY have North / South runways, 30 nm apart. When operating as Diass Approach (GOBD APP) and assuming the role of Departure, controllers must be mindful of simultaneous departures from both airports and keep aircraft separated.

GOBD (Blaise Diagne Intl) | v0.1

>> Go to this page.
>> Go to this page.

GOBD Approach

Rwy Fix Arrival Type Track Miles Approach Remarks

Approach Procedures

Rwy Approach Frequency Course GP Angle IAF Platform Remarks
01 ILS Z or LOC Z 110.10 IDS 006º 3.00º 8.6 DME IDS 3000 ft 16 DME Arc
ILS Y or LOC Y 110.10 IDS 006º 3.00º 8.6 DME IDS 3000 ft -
ILS X or LOC X 110.10 IDS 006º 3.00º DEPAL, GATRO or LIPEK 3000 ft -
RNAV (GNSS) - 006º 3.00º DEPAL, GATRO or LIPEK 3000 ft -
VOR Z 115.40 DS 006º 3.00º 8 DME DS 3000 ft 16 DME Arc
VOR Y 115.40 DS 006º 3.00º 8 DME DS 3000 ft -
19 RNAV (GNSS) - 186º 3.00º KOSOM, PIMLA or RUSMO 3000 ft -
VOR Z 115.40 DS 186º 3.00º 11 DME DS 3000 ft 16 DME Arc
VOR Y 115.40 DS 186º 3.00º 11 DME DS 3000 ft -

Holds

Name STAR Altitude Speed (IAS) Inbound CRS Turn Leg Time Remarks
DEPAL - 3000 ft to FL090 230 KT 006º Right 1 min Runway 01
KOSOM - 3000 ft to FL090 230 KT 186º Right 1 min Runway 19

Missed Approach Procedures

Rwy Approach Missed Approach Procedure Hold Remarks
01 ILS Z or LOC Z Climb on Runway Heading. At 2000 ft turn LEFT and climb to 3000 ft to join the DS VOR. Then join DEPAL via R-176 DS. DEPAL -
ILS Y or LOC Y
VOR Z or VOR Y
IlS X or LOC X Climb on Runway Heading. At BD502 turn LEFT and climb to 3000 ft and direct BD503. Then join GATRO.
RNAV (GNSS)
19 RNAV (GNSS) Climb on Runway Heading. At BD505 turn RIGHT and climb to 3000 ft and direct to BD506. Then join PIMLA. KOSOM -
VOR Z or VOR Y Climb on Runway Heading. At 2000 ft turn LEFT and climb to 3000 ft to join the DS VOR. Then join KOSOM via R-356 DS.

MSA and MRVA

Radio Aid Min Distance Max Distance Radial From Radial To MSA Remarks
DS 0 nm 25 nm 089º 029º 2000 ft -
029º 089º 3000 ft -

Diversions

ICAO Name Distance Longest Rwy Remarks
GOOY Leopold Sedar Senghor (Dakar, Senegal) 30 nm 3490 m -
GBYD Banjul Intl (Banjul, Gambia) 130 nm 3600 m -
GOGS Cap Skirring Intl (Cap Skirring, Senegal) 185 nm 2000 m -
GGOV Osvaldo Vieira (Bissau, Guinea-Bissau) 235 nm 3200 m -

GOBD (Blaise Diagne Intl) | v0.1

>> Go to this page.
>> Go to this page.

GOBD Coordination

GOBD & GOOY

  • GOBD Controllers, particularly Tower and Approach, should be mindful of simultaneous operations at GOBD and GOOY. In most cases, both airports will operate similar runway configurations (North/South). The fact that both airports are 30 nm apart allows for some maneuvering room, but during events, release management should be coordinated through Diass Approach.

GOBD & GVSC

  • For flights departing GOBD to airports at Cape Verde, GOBD Approach should coordinate with GVSC CTR or GVSC APP. In the absence of GOOO CTR and/or GVSC CTR, GOBD APP is authorized to handover traffic to GVSC APP.

GOBD & GOOO

GOBD & Oceanic

  • All GOBD departing flights that will cross Oceanic Airspace (GVSC or GOOO) will need request Oceanic Clearance on ground.

GOBD (Blaise Diagne Intl) | v0.1

GOBD (Blaise Diagne Intl) | v0.1

>> Go to this page.
>> Go to this page.

GOBD Pilot Briefing

Pilots taking part in events that depart from or arrive to GOBD should make themselves familiar with this briefing page as well as with all existing charts.

Overview

Departure Briefing

Clearance Delivery

* GOBD does not have a real world Delivery position but one has been created on VATSIM, particularly for busy events. If offline, contact Ground for Delivery Clearance.

* When requesting IFR clearance, please provide your Aircraft Type, Stand Number and ATIS letter.

Departure Procedures

* GOBD does not have structured departure procedures (SIDs) so you are expected to maintain runway heading until further instructions. Do not deviate from the runway heading after takeoff.

Arrival Briefing

Arrivals and Approaches

* As your flight is approaching GOBD, you should make yourself familiar with the existing Arrivals (STARs), Holding Points and Approaches.

* Be aware that GOBD has 3 distinct sets of ILS Approaches (X, Y and Z) and that they have distinct approach procedures. Controllers will inform you what Approach to expect based on an assigned Arrival.

* GOBD is a single runway airport, so during Arrival Events, you should expect to Hold.

* In case of Missed Approach, climb 3000 ft and maintain runway heading. You'll receive further instructions by the Controllers.

* During busy events, expect to vacate right on A3 if landing 01 or vacate left on A5 if landing 19. You might be requested by Tower to continue taxi after vacating the runway. Follow the instructions.

Parking and Ground Operations

* GOBD has plenty of parking positions across multiple aprons. Initial aircraft may be offered the Main Terminal Apron whilst subsequent aircraft will be directed to adjacent Aprons. There is limited parking availability at the Cargo Apron and due to its location on the airfield, you may be requested to taxi to a different parking position during busy events.

GOBD (Blaise Diagne Intl) | v0.1

>> Go to this page.

Senegal (GO) Airports | GOBD | GOOY
